title: eutriana hirsuta
intro: Eutriana hirsuta, also known as the "stinging tree," is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the Eutriana genus. The plant contains urticating hairs that can cause severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Medicinally, Eutriana hirsuta has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, with its purported medicinal properties attributed to the presence of triterpenoids and saponins. Eutriana hirsuta is often used in herbal preparations in the form of tinctures or infusions, although its use requires caution and should only be undertaken under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential to cause severe reactions.
